evidence: The paper explicitly states: "we first estimated the contribution of each genus feature to each model using the permutation-based approach from Altman et al. (2010) to assign a P value for each feature in each model." The main conclusions center on identifying "robustly well-predicted metabolites" via threshold-based classification (Spearman's rho > 0.3, FDR < 0.1), not on ranking features by importance magnitude. They identify "significant contributors" (P < 0.1) and "consistent contributors" (>50% of models), validating findings against known biological pathways (e.g., "Bacteroides...consistently contributed to models of several bile components...both in agreement with experimental findings"). The paper emphasizes metabolite predictability across datasets via cross-predictability analysis and model transfer rather than relying on feature importance magnitudes for conclusions.
